message:
feat(wal,pager): add deterministic per-mille shadow-compare sampling for parallel WAL Auto mode

In Auto mode, the parallel WAL previously had no way to run continuous
correctness verification — you either ran full ShadowCompare (2x work)
or nothing. This makes it impractical to verify lane-staging correctness
in production.

Adds parallel_wal_should_shadow_compare() — a deterministic sampling
gate that uses the batch sequence number modulo a configurable per-mille
rate to decide whether each batch gets shadow-compared. For example,
shadow_compare_per_mille=2 verifies ~0.2% of batches, giving ongoing
confidence with negligible overhead.

- parallel_wal.rs: new public parallel_wal_should_shadow_compare()
  function with per-mille parameter, plus unit tests for boundary values
  (0 = never, 1000 = always, partial rates)
- lib.rs: re-export the new function
- pager.rs: replace the hard-coded mode enum match with a call to the
  sampling function, using the configured per-mille rate
- E2E test: add auto-sampled run with shadow_compare_per_mille=1000
  confirming clean verdicts and row parity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
